name: Mee Krob
recipeIngredient: 6 ounces Med shrimp, shelled and deveined
Whole boned chicken breast, cut into slices
4 green onions, trimmed and cut into 1-inch lengths
recipeInstructions:
1. Cover the tamarind pulp with 3/4 cup hot water. Crush and break up the pulp using a fork, then let it stand for 20 minutes. Strain the mixture through a fine mesh strainer to collect about half a cup of tamarind liquid.
2. Heat oil in a wok or deep saucepan to 375°F (190°C). In small batches, add rice stick noodles and fry until they puff up within seconds. Remove with a slotted spoon or strainer and drain on paper towels. Repeat with remaining noodles.
3. If preparing the Crispy Egg Lace, do so at this time (see below).
4. When the noodles and egg lace are done, pour off all but 2 tablespoons of oil from the wok. Reheat the wok and oil over medium-high heat.
5. Add shrimp and chicken; stir-fry for 1 minute or until shrimp turn bright orange and chicken is white. Remove and set aside.
6. Add shallots, garlic, minced chiles, and half the lime zest to the hot wok; stir-fry until soft but not browned (about 1 minute).
7. Add tomato paste and sugar. Cook, stirring constantly, until sugar becomes a dark crimson red with a sticky consistency. It should pull away from the wok into a thick mass. This is just short of the caramelized state (about 3 to 4 minutes). Be careful not to burn the mixture.
8. Immediately add the reserved tamarind liquid and fish sauce, reduce heat to medium-low, and simmer together for 1 minute.
9. Add lime juice, reserved chicken-shrimp mixture, green onions, and remaining lime zest; toss just enough to heat through.
10. Remove from heat. Add one-third of the fried rice stick noodles to the sauce. Gently crush and toss with sauce to coat. Repeat with another third of noodles. Add the last third of noodles if there is enough sauce to coat. Toss in coriander leaves.
11. Serve by mounding noodles on a platter, arranging Crispy Egg Lace (broken into smaller pieces), and bean sprouts.
Crispy Egg Lace:
1. After frying noodles, skim leftover bits from the wok. Keep oil hot.
2. Beat eggs with salt in a bowl.
3. When oil reaches 375°F (190°C), hold a medium-fine mesh skimmer over the oil; gently pour half the eggs through. Let the eggs drip into the oil in a circular fashion, deep-frying for 30 seconds or until lightly brown and crisp. Turn over to brown evenly. It should have an irregular lacy shape.
4. Remove from heat and drain on paper towels. Keep warm in an oven.
5. Break into smaller pieces and serve.
